chapter_title.en: Chapter 17 – What are rejected, from the witnesses

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/251)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/251)
text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ السَّائِلِ الَّذِي يَسْأَلُ فِي كَفِّهِ هَلْ تُقْبَلُ شَهَادَتُهُ فَقَالَ كَانَ أَبِي ( عليه السلام ) لَا يَقْبَلُ شَهَادَتَهُ إِذَا سَأَلَ فِي كَفِّهِ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Al Amaraky Bin Ali,

(It has been narrated) from Ali son of Ja’far<sup>asws</sup>, from his brother<sup>asws</sup> Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup>, said, ‘I asked him<sup>asws</sup> about the beggar who begs by his palm, would his testimony be accepted?’ So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘His testimony is not acceptable when he begs by his palm’.
en.sarwar: 14. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from al-‘Amrakiy ibn Ali from Ali ibn Ja‘far from his brother who has narrated the following: “I once asked abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a beggar man who extends his hand for begging, if his testimony is acceptable. He (the Imam) said, ‘My father would not accept his testimony if he extended his hand for begging.’”
